channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabweans repatriated from South Africa face possible re-entry ban
item.description: Cape Times Foreign nationals who voluntarily agreed to be repatriated from Cape Town to the Beitbridge border have expressed dismay after learning they would be declared undesirable and barred from re-entering South Africa for up to five years. Since Sunday, the Department of Home Affairs office in Epping has served as the processing centre for [...] The post Zimbabweans repatriated from South Africa face possible re-entry ban appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe moves to digitise mining claims under new cadastre system
item.description: ZIMBABWE is pressing ahead with plans to establish a transparent digital register of all mining claims across the country as part of efforts to strengthen governance and attract fresh investment into the mining sector. Mines and Mining Development Minister Polite Kambamura on Tuesday briefed Cabinet on progress made towards implementing the Zimbabwe Mining Cadastre Registration [...] The post Zimbabwe moves to digitise mining claims under new cadastre system appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: AMH, ZimInd editor Zaba removed from remand as state withdraws charges
item.description: Zimbabwe Independent editor Faith Zaba and her employer, Alpha Media Holdings (AMH), were removed from remand after the National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) withdrew criminal charges against them before plea, bringing the prosecution to an end. Harare magistrate Aplonia Marutya ordered that Zaba and AMH be removed from remand after the state formally withdrew the charges. [...] The post AMH, ZimInd editor Zaba removed from remand as state withdraws charges appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe complete biggest ever Test win after thrashing Bangladesh in Harare
item.description: SABC Sport Innocent Kaia struck a maiden century before the bowlers starred as Zimbabwe recorded their biggest-ever Test victory after thrashing Bangladesh by an innings and 85 runs in Harare. Zimbabwe took the early ascendancy in the contest when Bangladesh were bowled out for just 140 in the first innings with 20-year-old left-arm seamer Newman [...] The post Zimbabwe complete biggest ever Test win after thrashing Bangladesh in Harare appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Building societies’ lowest-paid workers to earn US$660 after new wage deal
item.description: THE Commercial Workers Union of Zimbabwe (CWUZ) has secured a significant wage increase for employees in the building societies sector following the successful conclusion of a new Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA). The agreement was reached between CWUZ, representing non-managerial employees, and the National Commercial Employers Association of Zimbabwe. Under the new salary structure, the lowest-paid [...] The post Building societies’ lowest-paid workers to earn US$660 after new wage deal appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Parliament finalises bill to extend presidential term

item.title: Ford rehires human engineers after AI fails to match quality checks
item.description: Ford says it has hired back some human engineers after AI failed to match their skills and experience. In a bid to reap the benefits of the tech, which developers claim can cut costs and boost productivity, the US carmaker adopted it across some parts of its operations including for quality checks. But, according to [...] The post Ford rehires human engineers after AI fails to match quality checks appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Zimbabwe Steps Up Repatriation Of Citizens From South Africa By Deploying More Buses
item.description: Government steps up Zimbabwean repatriation from South Africa as more buses deployed The Zimbabwean government says it has intensified its repatriation programme for citizens returning from South Africa, deploying more buses from Cape Town, Gqeberha (Port Elizabeth), Johannesburg and Durban while assuring returnees that the operation is being fully funded. The latest update, issued by [...] The post Zimbabwe Steps Up Repatriation Of Citizens From South Africa By Deploying More Buses appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: FBC Bank reaffirms commitment to supporting the growth and success of SMEs @ZSE_ZW
item.description: Judith Phiri FINANCIAL institution, FBC Bank Limited has reaffirmed its commitment to supporting the growth and success of small and medium enterprises (SMEs), who are the backbone of Zimbabwe’s economy. With approximately 3,4 million entities, SMEs contribute over 60 percent of the country’s gross domestic product (GDP) and employing roughly 4,8 million people full-time. Speaking at the World SME Day: Commemoration Seminar in Bulawayo on Tuesday, FBC Bank Managing Director, Mr Mubaiwa Mubayiwa said entrepreneurs, innovators and business leaders, were indispensable partners in driving inclusive economic growth, job creation and national development. “Today, we celebrate your contribution and reaffirm our commitment to supporting the growth and success of SMEs. As FBC Bank, we recognise that a vibrant SME sector is fundamental to driving inclusive socio-economic growth,” he said. “We have long understood the significance of supporting this critical segment of the economy, which is why we have established a dedicated Business Banking Unit focused on addressing the unique needs of SMEs.” He said through tailored financial solutions, advisory support and strategic partnerships, they remain committed to empowering entrepreneurs at every stage of their business journey. Mr Mubayiwa said the gathering reaffirms that commitment and reflects their shared ambition to help Zimbabwean SMEs build resilient, competitive and future-ready enterprises. He added: “We meet at a time when the global economy is undergoing profound transformation. Technological advancements, changing consumer behaviour, evolving regulatory environments and increasing market interconnectedness are reshaping the way businesses operate. The Fourth Industrial Revolution is no longer a future concept; it is now our present reality.” Mr Mubayiwa said businesses across the world are embracing digital technologies, data analytics, automation and artificial intelligence (AI) to improve efficiency, enhance customer experience, optimise decision-making and unlock new growth opportunities. He said AI presents a unique opportunity to leapfrog traditional barriers to growth and improve productivity without requiring significant capital investment. “For SMEs, these developments represent significant opportunities. The businesses that will thrive in the coming decade will be those that embrace innovation, leverage technology and remain agile in responding to an ever-changing marketplace. “The SME sector remains one of the most important pillars of Zimbabwe’s economy. The sector is estimated to encompass over 3.4 million enterprises and contributes approximately US$8.6 billion to Zimbabwe’s GDP,” he said. “SMEs are also the lifeblood of our economy; creating employment, driving innovation, supporting livelihoods and sustaining communities across the country. As FBC Bank, we have a special appreciation of the entrepreneurial journey because our own story reflects the power of enterprise, perseverance and vision.” Mr Mubayiwa said their institution was established in 1997 as First Banking Corporation with two branches in Harare and Zvishavane. While, over the years, the FBC brand has evolved into one of Zimbabwe’s leading integrated financial services groups, comprising eight strategic business units. “Our transformation from a modest banking institution into a diversified financial services powerhouse demonstrates what can be achieved through innovation, customer focus, prudent investment and an unwavering commitment to growth. “This journey mirrors the aspirations of many SMEs represented here today. It is a reminder that every large enterprise was once a small business. As FBC Bank, our purpose extends beyond providing financial services,” he said. He said they were committed to empowering SMEs to evolve from small enterprises into sustainable and high-growth businesses that create jobs, drive innovation and contribute meaningfully to national development. Mr Mubayiwa said their ambition was to support the journey from start-up to scale-up, from informal enterprise to formal business and ultimately from local success stories to companies capable of listing on the Zimbabwe Stock Exchange (ZSE) and competing on regional and global platforms. The event hosted by FBC Bank ran under the theme: “Future- Ready SMEs: Unlocking Growth Through AI and Inclusive Digital Finance.” (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: Mnangagwa Regime In Desperate Damage Control As Zimbabweans Return Home From SA
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| The Government of Zimbabwe has moved to contain growing fallout from the worsening return of Zimbabweans from South... Continue reading "Mnangagwa Regime In Desperate Damage Control As Zimbabweans Return Home From SA" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: The Diaspora Divorce Curse: Why Zimbabwean Marriages are Collapsing the Moment They Leave The Country
item.description: For many Zimbabweans, the dream of a better life abroad—in the United Kingdom, Canada, or Australia—is a powerful motivator. It promises economic stability, improved opportunities, and a brighter future for families. Yet, beneath this shimmering facade of hope, a silent crisis is unfolding: the “diaspora divorce” curse. Thousands of Zimbabwean marriages are crumbling shortly after [...] The post The Diaspora Divorce Curse: Why Zimbabwean Marriages are Collapsing the Moment They Leave The Country first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: NetOne Backs 500km Walkathon to Tackle Zimbabwe’s Drug Crisis @263Chat @NetOneCellular
item.description: A 23-day walk from Zimbabwe’s southern border to the capital is drawing attention to the country’s growing drug and substance abuse crisis with organisers hoping every kilometre covered will encourage more young people to choose recovery over addiction. The campaign, dubbed “Together We Rise, United [...] The post NetOne Backs 500km Walkathon to Tackle Zimbabwe’s Drug Crisis appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Proplastics Accelerates Regional Expansion as Export Sales Surge 139%
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwean plastic piping manufacturer Proplastics Limited significantly expanded its regional footprint during the first five months of 2026, with export sales soaring 139 percent as the company strengthened its position in regional markets despite policy challenges affecting exporters. The strong export performance increased exports’ contribution to total group revenue to six percent, up [...] The post Proplastics Accelerates Regional Expansion as Export Sales Surge 139% appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Property Market Enters New Era as Investors Shift Focus Beyond Land Speculation
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe’s property market is undergoing a significant transformation, with 2026 marking a decisive shift away from the long-held investment strategy of simply acquiring land and waiting for prices to appreciate. For decades, the country’s real estate sector has largely been driven by speculative land ownership. Investors bought undeveloped residential stands, commercial land and [...] The post Zimbabwe’s Property Market Enters New Era as Investors Shift Focus Beyond Land Speculation appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
